description: sends out the current value when a voice is started (note-on is received)

parameterGroups.label: Input
parameterGroups.rationale: The value to send when triggered by a voice start event.

gaps.question: voice_bang does not have IsProcessingHiseEvent. From control-infrastructure Pattern 5, it fires on note-on via handleHiseEvent. How does it receive MIDI events without IsProcessingHiseEvent? Does it use a different trigger mechanism (e.g., reset() callback on voice start)?
gaps.context: The trigger mechanism determines when the output fires -- on MIDI note-on or on voice reset.

gaps.question: Does it send the current Value at the moment of the trigger, or a fixed 1.0 bang signal? The description says 'sends out the current value' suggesting Value is the payload.
gaps.context: Users need to know whether the Value parameter is the payload or just a trigger threshold.

gaps.question: IsPolyphonic is false in cppProperties, but the description mentions 'when a voice is started'. Does it work in polyphonic containers? Does it fire once per voice or once globally?
gaps.context: Polyphonic behaviour is critical for understanding voice-start use cases.

notes: voice_bang sends its current Value when a voice starts. Despite describing voice-aware behaviour, IsPolyphonic is false in cppProperties. It also lacks IsProcessingHiseEvent, suggesting it may use the reset() callback (which fires on voice start) rather than handleHiseEvent. Pattern 5 from control-infrastructure shows it checking isNoteOn() in handleHiseEvent, so there may be a discrepancy.
